message:
fix(freeze): handle IEEE 754 NaN in NaN-boxing scheme

Extend the previous Infinity fix to also recognize actual IEEE 754 NaN
values. Both use exponent 0x7FF, but differ in mantissa:

- Infinity: exponent=0x7FF, mantissa=0
- NaN: exponent=0x7FF, mantissa!=0, typically quiet bit (bit 51) set

The canonical quiet NaN has bit pattern 0x7FF8000000000000, which has
the same upper 16 bits as our QNAN base (no tag bits). We distinguish
actual NaN from our tagged values by checking tag_bits == 0x7FF80000.

Updated isFloat() and toJSValuePtr() in both wasm32 and native paths
to correctly identify and convert NaN values.
